00029 
00030 /*
00031  * Driver for full-screen console.
00032  *
00033  * As of this writing the full-screen console is not supported in
00034  * System/161, so this driver is untested and probably broken.
00035  */
00036 #include <types.h>
00037 #include <lib.h>
00038 #include <spinlock.h>
00039 #include <platform/bus.h>
00040 #include <lamebus/lscreen.h>
00041 #include "autoconf.h"
00042 
00043 /* Registers (offsets within slot) */
00044 #define LSCR_REG_POSN    0   /* Cursor position */
00045 #define LSCR_REG_SIZE    4   /* Display size */
00046 #define LSCR_REG_CHAR    8   /* Character in */
00047 #define LSCR_REG_RIRQ    12  /* Read interrupt status */
00048 
00049 /* Bits in the IRQ registers */
00050 #define LSCR_IRQ_ENABLE  1
00051 #define LSCR_IRQ_ACTIVE  2
00052 
00053 /* Offset within slot of screen buffer */
00054 #define LSCR_SCREEN      32768
00055 
00056 /* Convert a 32-bit X/Y pair to X and Y coordinates. */ 
00057 static
00058 inline
00059 void
00060 splitxy(uint32_t xy, unsigned *x, unsigned *y)
00061 {
00062         *x = xy >> 16;
00063         *y = xy & 0xffff;
00064 }
00065 
00066 /* Convert X and Y coordinates to a single 32-bit value. */
00067 static
00068 inline
00069 uint32_t
00070 mergexy(unsigned x, unsigned y)
00071 {
00072         uint32_t val = x;
00073 
00074         return (val << 16) | y;
00075 }
00076 
00078 
00079 /*
00080  * Interrupt handler.
00081  */
00082 void
00083 lscreen_irq(void *vsc)
00084 {
00085         struct lscreen_softc *sc = vsc;
00086         uint32_t ch, x;
00087 
00088         spinlock_acquire(&sc->ls_lock);
00089 
00090         x = bus_read_register(sc->ls_busdata, sc->ls_buspos, LSCR_REG_RIRQ);
00091         if (x & LSCR_IRQ_ACTIVE) {
00092                 ch = bus_read_register(sc->ls_busdata, sc->ls_buspos,
00093                                        LSCR_REG_CHAR);
00094                 bus_write_register(sc->ls_busdata, sc->ls_buspos,
00095                                    LSCR_REG_RIRQ, LSCR_IRQ_ENABLE);
00096 
00097                 spinlock_release(&sc->ls_lock);
00098                 if (sc->ls_input) {
00099                         sc->ls_input(sc->ls_devdata, ch);
00100                 }
00101         }
00102         else {
00103                 spinlock_release(&sc->ls_lock);
00104         }
00105 }
00106 
00108 
00109 /*
00110  * Handle a newline on the screen.
00111  */
00112 static
00113 void
00114 lscreen_newline(struct lscreen_softc *sc)
00115 {
00116         if (sc->ls_cy >= sc->ls_height-1) {
00117                 /*
00118                  * Scroll
00119                  */
00120 
00121                 memmove(sc->ls_screen, sc->ls_screen + sc->ls_width,
00122                         sc->ls_width * (sc->ls_height-1));
00123                 bzero(sc->ls_screen + sc->ls_width * (sc->ls_height-1),
00124                       sc->ls_width);
00125         }
00126         else {
00127                 sc->ls_cy++;
00128         }
00129         sc->ls_cx=0;
00130 }
00131 
00132 /*
00133  * Handle a printable character being written to the screen.
00134  */
00135 static
00136 void
00137 lscreen_char(struct lscreen_softc *sc, int ch)
00138 {
00139         if (sc->ls_cx >= sc->ls_width) {
00140                 lscreen_newline(sc);
00141         }
00142 
00143         sc->ls_screen[sc->ls_cy*sc->ls_width + sc->ls_cx] = ch;
00144         sc->ls_cx++;
00145 }
00146 
00147 /*
00148  * Send a character to the screen.
00149  * This should probably know about backspace and tab.
00150  */
00151 void
00152 lscreen_write(void *vsc, int ch)
00153 {
00154         struct lscreen_softc *sc = vsc;
00155         int ccx, ccy;
00156 
00157         spinlock_acquire(&sc->ls_lock);
00158 
00159         switch (ch) {
00160             case '\n': lscreen_newline(sc); break;
00161             default: lscreen_char(sc, ch); break;
00162         }
00163 
00164         /*
00165          * ccx/ccy = corrected cursor position
00166          * (The cursor marks the next space text will appear in. But
00167          * at the very end of the line, it should not move off the edge.)
00168          */
00169         ccx = sc->ls_cx;
00170         ccy = sc->ls_cy;
00171         if (ccx==sc->ls_width) {
00172                 ccx--;
00173         }
00174 
00175         /* Set the cursor position */
00176         bus_write_register(sc->ls_busdata, sc->ls_buspos,
00177                            LSCR_REG_POSN, mergexy(ccx, ccy));
00178 
00179         spinlock_release(&sc->ls_lock);
00180 }
00181 
00183 
00184 /*
00185  * Setup routine called by autoconf.c when an lscreen is found.
00186  */
00187 int
00188 config_lscreen(struct lscreen_softc *sc, int lscreenno)
00189 {
00190         uint32_t val;
00191 
00192         (void)lscreenno;
00193 
00194         spinlock_init(&sc->ls_lock);
00195 
00196         /*
00197          * Enable interrupting.
00198          */
00199 
00200         bus_write_register(sc->ls_busdata, sc->ls_buspos,
00201                            LSCR_REG_RIRQ, LSCR_IRQ_ENABLE);
00202 
00203         /*
00204          * Get screen size.
00205          */
00206         val = bus_read_register(sc->ls_busdata, sc->ls_buspos,
00207                                 LSCR_REG_SIZE);
00208         splitxy(val, &sc->ls_width, &sc->ls_height);
00209 
00210         /*
00211          * Get cursor position.
00212          */
00213         val = bus_read_register(sc->ls_busdata, sc->ls_buspos,
00214                                 LSCR_REG_POSN);
00215         splitxy(val, &sc->ls_cx, &sc->ls_cy);
00216 
00217         /*
00218          * Get a pointer to the memory-mapped screen area.
00219          */
00220         sc->ls_screen = bus_map_area(sc->ls_busdata, sc->ls_buspos,
00221                                      LSCR_SCREEN);
00222 
00223         return 0;
00224 }
00225
